alignments.preprint_claim_text: AUC and the normalized concordant partial AUC are balanced average accuracy, which is a new finding.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new interpretation of AUC as balanced average accuracy is relevant to individuals rather than pairs.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the reinterpretation of AUC but emphasizes its relevance to individuals, and the reported certainty is somewhat lower than the preprint's categorical phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The normalized partial AUC is average sensitivity, and the normalized horizontal partial AUC is average specificity.
alignments.rationale: The published paper does not restate these specific equivalences for normalized partial AUC variants, so the explicit claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep ROC analysis can improve model selection in some cases and provide interpretation and assurance for patients in each risk group.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The utility of the deep ROC analysis method is confirmed through evaluation in three case studies.
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the general claim about improving model selection and patient-level assurance to demonstrated utility in three case studies, providing stronger empirical support but reduced generality.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed traffic surveillance camera calibration method is based on the detection of pairs of vanishing points associated with vehicles in traffic footage.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed traffic surveillance camera calibration method is based on the detection of pairs of vanishing points associated with vehicles in traffic footage.
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication texts are identical in content and scope, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A CNN is proposed that outputs heatmaps representing the positions of vanishing points using diamond space parametrization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A CNN is proposed that outputs heatmaps representing the positions of vanishing points using diamond space parametrization.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is stated the same way in both versions with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method establishes scene geometry by estimating the focal length of the camera and the orientation of the road plane from detected pairs of vanishing points.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method establishes scene geometry by estimating the focal length of the camera and the orientation of the road plane from detected pairs of vanishing points.
alignments.rationale: The result is presented identically in both preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieves competitive results on the BrnoCarPark dataset while having fewer requirements than the current state of the art approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method achieves competitive results on the BrnoCarPark dataset while having fewer requirements than the current state of the art approach.
alignments.rationale: The performance claim and comparative statement are unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel cross-view transformer method can transfer information between unregistered views at the level of spatial feature maps.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel cross-view transformer method can transfer information between unregistered views at the level of spatial feature maps.
alignments.rationale: The core contribution statement is identical in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The cross-view transformer outperforms a baseline model that joins feature vectors after global pooling on multi-view mammography and chest X-ray datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The cross-view transformer outperforms a baseline model that joins feature vectors after global pooling on multi-view mammography and chest X-ray datasets.
alignments.rationale: The reported empirical result matches exactly between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining views at a global feature level is limited when registration is not possible.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining views at a global feature level is limited to joining feature vectors after global pooling when registration is not possible.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the exact manner of the limitation (joining after global pooling), which clarifies and slightly narrows the original, more general statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study investigates how process designers perceive the usefulness and ease of use of CMMN and DCR based on the Technology Acceptance Model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study investigates how process designers perceive the usefulness and ease of use of CMMN and DCR based on the Technology Acceptance Model.
alignments.rationale: The investigative objective is described the same way in both versions; differences are only in paper-role labeling.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results show that the perception of participants can change by receiving feedback, despite being well trained.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The perception of participants can change slightly by receiving feedback, although the change is not significant due to being well trained.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds magnitude ('slightly') and statistical significance context, which clarifies and tempers the original assertion about change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both CMMN and DCR have an acceptable level of perceived usefulness and ease of use according to the study's findings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both CMMN and DCR have an acceptable level of perceived usefulness and ease of use according to the reliability tested using Cronbach’s alpha.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the specific reliability test (Cronbach’s alpha) as evidence, clarifying the basis for the acceptability claim without changing its substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The adaptive deep density approximation strategy based on KRnet (ADDA-KR) effectively solves the steady-state Fokker-Planck equations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The adaptive deep density approximation strategy based on KRnet (ADDA-KR) provides effective solution candidates for the steady-state Fokker-Planck equations.
alignments.rationale: The publication softens the claim from 'effectively solves' to 'provides effective solution candidates,' reducing the strength of the claim and narrowing its scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: KRnet provides a family of probability density functions that serve as effective solution candidates for the Fokker-Planck equations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method can efficiently estimate general high-dimensional density functions.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the KRnet-related claim into a general capability to estimate high-dimensional densities; this generalizes the original specific wording about solution candidates.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ADDA-KR has a weaker dependence on dimensionality compared to traditional computational approaches.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ADDA-KR has a weaker dependence on dimensionality than traditional computational approaches.
alignments.rationale: The content is the same but the publication presents this as a higher-confidence/result-level claim (preprint was medium certainty, publication is high), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The adaptive sampling procedure developed for ADDA-KR generates effective stochastic collocation points for approximating the Fokker-Planck equation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An adaptive sampling procedure is developed to obtain effective stochastic collocation points for the approximation of the Fokker-Planck equation.
alignments.rationale: The procedure is present in both versions with the same intent, but the publication reports it with lower stated certainty than the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy and efficiency of ADDA-KR are validated through numerical experiments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method can efficiently estimate general high-dimensional density functions.
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts efficient estimation capability (likely supported by experiments) rather than explicitly stating 'validated through numerical experiments,' so the validation claim is reframed/clarified rather than fully retained verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Deep Dense Multi-Scale Network (DDMSNet) effectively removes snow from images by utilizing semantic and geometric priors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Deep Dense Multi-Scale Network (DDMSNet) effectively removes snow from images by utilizing semantic and depth priors.
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces 'geometric priors' with 'depth priors,' a terminology change that revises the type of prior used while keeping the overall claim of effective snow removal.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating semantic and geometric maps as input enhances the snow removal process by providing strong prior information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating semantic and depth maps as input enhances the performance of snow removal in images.
alignments.rationale: The publication modifies 'geometric maps' to 'depth maps' and frames the benefit as enhanced performance, revising the specific prior type while preserving the core conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments on public synthetic and real-world snowy images demonstrate that DDMSNet offers superior results compared to existing methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms existing techniques in both quantitative and qualitative assessments.
alignments.rationale: The publication gives more detail about the evaluations (quantitative and qualitative) while preserving the original superiority/result claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of a self-attention mechanism in DDMSNet contributes to the production of final clean images.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claim set does not mention self-attention or its contribution, indicating this specific claim was removed or de-emphasized.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Dual Attention-in-Attention Model (DAiAM) effectively removes both rain streaks and raindrops simultaneously.

alignments.rationale: The published claim matches the preprint claim exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Differential-driven Dual Attention-in-Attention Model (D-DAiAM) improves deraining results by addressing unsatisfying regions with a 'heavy-to-light' scheme.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Federated learning is effective when dealing with independent and identically distributed datasets but struggles with non-iid datasets.

alignments.rationale: The publication drops the positive note about iid cases and strengthens the critique by stating federated learning 'fails' to handle distribution shifts, broadening and amplifying the original concern.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed dynamically optimized personal deep learning scheme based on blockchain and federated learning achieves higher accuracy compared to traditional federated and personalized learning approaches.

alignments.rationale: The published claim preserves the higher accuracy result and adds an extra measured benefit (faster convergence), clarifying the empirical outcomes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The innovative smart contract implemented in the blockchain allows distributed edge devices to reach a consensus on the optimal weights of personalized models.

alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the consensus-on-weights functionality but generalizes the implementation (the explicit mention of an "innovative smart contract" is not retained), so the claim is slightly revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Trust concerns among unacquainted devices and security concerns with the centralized aggregator pose additional challenges to federated learning.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the preprint's explicit statements about trust and centralized-aggregator security concerns, so this limitation is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Local Collaborative Autoencoders (LOCA) is a generalized local latent factor framework that effectively captures diverse user preferences.

alignments.rationale: The publication refines the original general claim by framing LOCA as the first framework under a specific assumption and by tying it to neural recommendation models, making the contribution more specific.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LOCA employs a novel sub-community discovery method that maximizes the coverage of a union of local models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel method of discovering a sub-community is developed to maximize the coverage of local models.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same methodological contribution; however its certainty is reported as medium rather than high, so confidence is slightly tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that LOCA is scalable and outperforms state-of-the-art models on several public benchmarks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LOCA is highly scalable.
alignments.rationale: The preprint bundled scalability and outperforming claims; this alignment maps the scalability part to Q2 but drops the explicit outperforming element (handled by another publication claim), so the content is narrowed/revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LOCA achieves improvements of 2.99~4.70% in Recall and 1.02~7.95% in NDCG compared to existing models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LOCA achieves state-of-the-art results by outperforming existing AE-based and local latent factor models on several large-scale public benchmarks.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the outperforming result but expresses it more generally and specifies the baseline families; numeric specifics from the preprint are omitted, so the claim is clarified and tied to particular baselines.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed end-to-end solution for in-memory malicious activity detection leverages machine learning capabilities based on unique run-time logs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel end-to-end solution for in-memory malicious activity detection leverages machine learning capabilities based on unique run-time logs.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim with essentially identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This solution achieves reduced overhead and false positives compared to existing malware detection approaches.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This solution achieves reduced overhead and false positives.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the performance claim (reduced overhead and false positives) with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implementation of the solution for Windows-based systems incorporates multidisciplinary knowledge from malware research, machine learning, and operating system internals.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include this implementation/disciplinary-integration claim, indicating it was omitted from the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental evaluation of the solution yielded promising results.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This solution achieves reduced overhead and false positives.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's vague "promising results" is grounded in the publication by specifying reduced overhead and false positives; the publication presents this more strongly (higher certainty), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The solution can be extended to thwart future sophisticated malware attempts to bypass detection.
alignments.rationale: The speculative extension claim in the preprint does not appear in the publication, so it was removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach achieves real-time instance segmentation of instruments at 37 frames per second.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach achieves real-time instance segmentation of instruments at over 45 frames per second.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a substantially higher runtime figure (over 45 fps vs 37 fps), which revises the numeric claim and strengthens (broadens/amplifies) the performance assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The addition of attention mechanisms to the YOLACT architecture improves accuracy on the ROBUST-MIS dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The addition of attention mechanisms to the YOLACT architecture improves accuracy for instance segmentation on the ROBUST-MIS dataset.
alignments.rationale: Both versions state the same methodological improvement and dataset impact with consistent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates competitive performance compared to the winner of the 2019 ROBUST-MIS challenge, with robustness scores of 0.313 MI_DSC and 0.338 MI_NSD.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates competitive performance compared to the winner of the 2019 ROBUST-MIS challenge, with robustness scores of 0.313 ML_DSC and 0.338 MLNSD.
alignments.rationale: The numeric robustness scores and competitive conclusion are preserved; minor metric label differences do not alter the substantive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This overview paper addresses the lack of human activity recognition (HAR) overview papers focusing on healthcare applications that use wearable sensors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This overview paper addresses the lack of human activity recognition (HAR) overview papers focusing on healthcare applications that use wearable sensors.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution claim verbatim with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The projects presented include early mobility identification of human activities for intensive care unit (ICU) patients and gait analysis of Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD) patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The projects presented include early mobility identification of human activities for intensive care unit (ICU) patients and gait analysis of Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD) patients.
alignments.rationale: The project descriptions are the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Essential components of designing HAR systems include sensor factors, AI model selection, and feature engineering.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Essential components of designing HAR systems include sensor factors, AI model selection, and feature engineering.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about essential components is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper highlights the challenges of healthcare-oriented HAR systems and proposes several research opportunities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper highlights the challenges of healthcare-oriented HAR systems and proposes several research opportunities.
alignments.rationale: The challenge/opportunity framing is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach locates approximate solutions $x'$ and $y'$ to $\text{min}_{x'}f(x', y)$ and $\text{max}_{y'}f(x, y')$ at a given point $(x, y)$ and updates $(x, y)$ toward these approximate solutions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach locates approximate solutions x and y to minx f(x, y) and maxy f(x, y) at a given point (x, y) and updates (x, y) toward these approximate solutions.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this algorithmic result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: On locally strong convex--concave smooth functions, we derive conditions on $\tau$ to exhibit linear convergence to a local saddle point.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We derive conditions on the learning rate to exhibit linear convergence to a local saddle point on locally strong convex-concave smooth functions.
alignments.rationale: The theoretical convergence claim is maintained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This reveals a possible shortcoming of recently developed robust adversarial reinforcement learning algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This reveals a possible shortcoming of recently developed robust adversarial reinforcement learning algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about potential shortcomings is preserved with the same characterization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We develop a heuristic approach to adapt the learning rate $\tau$ derivative-free and implement zero-order and first-order minimization algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We develop a heuristic approach to adapt derivative-free and implement zero-order and first-order minimization algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The contribution describing the heuristic adaptation approach and implementations is retained with effectively the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical experiments are conducted to show the tightness of the theoretical results as well as the usefulness of the $\tau$ adaptation mechanism.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical experiments are conducted to show the tightness of the theoretical results as well as the usefulness of the adaptation mechanism.
alignments.rationale: The experimental claim is preserved; the publication lists this with higher certainty (high vs medium), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed neural network (NN) based tuning procedure allows users to prioritize between selected quality criteria such as control and observation errors. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed neural network (NN) based tuning procedure allows prioritization between selected quality criteria such as control and observation errors. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication wording is effectively identical in meaning and intent to the preprint, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The designed NN provides an accurate assessment of the control system performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The designed NN provides an accurate assessment of the control system performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim appears verbatim between preprint and publication and retains the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The tuning procedure produces near-optimal ESO gains within seconds using an estimated state from a single closed-loop experiment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tuning procedure produces near-optimal ESO gains within seconds using an estimated state from a single closed-loop experiment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this technical result without substantive change in wording or emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Proper tuning of ESO parameters is necessary to ensure a good quality of estimated quantities and impacts the overall performance of the robust control structure. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Proper tuning of ESO parameters is necessary to ensure a good quality of estimated quantities and impacts the overall performance of the robust control structure. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is retained exactly in the publication with the same scope and expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hybrid human-artificial intelligence (H-AI) integrates both human intelligence and AI into one unity, forming a new enhanced intelligence. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hybrid human-artificial intelligence (H-AI) integrates both human intelligence and AI into one unity, forming a new enhanced intelligence. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is preserved verbatim between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: H-AI shows advantages in dealing with social problems that AI cannot surpass. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: H-AI shows some advantages over conventional AI in dealing with social problems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication weakens the original absolute phrasing ('AI cannot surpass') to a more modest claim of 'some advantages', indicating a revision and narrowed claim strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper proposes a holistic framework of social computing that combines H-AI, consisting of four layers: object layer, base layer, analysis layer, and application layer. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed architecture of H-AI consists of three layers: object layer, intelligent processing layer, and application layer. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication alters the proposed architecture (four layers -> three layers), a substantive revision of the claim's content; certainty remains similar but the scope of the architecture is narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The conventional technologies of AI have drawbacks in dealing with more complicated and dynamic problems. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of H-AI is motivated by the typical challenges AI faces in social computing. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the criticism as motivation by 'typical challenges' rather than asserting broad drawbacks, clarifying and softening the original limitation statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The latest research progresses of AI in social computing are reviewed to summarize typical challenges faced by AI. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of H-AI is motivated by the typical challenges AI faces in social computing. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The explicit claim of conducting a review in the preprint is not retained; the publication instead presents challenges as motivation. This is a revision in emphasis and likely a condensation of the review claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The artificial neural network model performed well above 85% levels for identifying images of asteroid resonant arguments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ANN model achieved over 85 percent accuracy in identifying images of asteroid resonant arguments based on standard metrics like accuracy, precision, and recall. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the >85% performance claim with additional metric clarification but no substantive change in the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model allows for the identification of the orbital type of all numbered asteroids in the region. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study successfully predicted the orbital status of all multi-opposition asteroids in the area using supervised machine learning methods optimized through genetic algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The target population changed from 'all numbered asteroids' to 'multi-opposition asteroids,' which narrows the claim about which objects were identified, though the overall predictive result remains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The M1:2 resonance mainly affects the orbits of the Massalia, Nysa, and Vesta asteroid families. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The M1:2 resonance primarily affects the orbits of the Massalia, Nysa, and Vesta asteroid families. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is retained with equivalent wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Supervised machine learning methods optimized through genetic algorithms were used to predict the orbital status of all multi-opposition asteroids in the area. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study successfully predicted the orbital status of all multi-opposition asteroids in the area using supervised machine learning methods optimized through genetic algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication elevates the methods claim to an explicit successful result and expresses it with higher certainty, clarifying and amplifying the original methodological statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Photoacoustic tomography (PAT) has the potential to recover morphological and functional tissue properties with high spatial resolution. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Photoacoustic tomography (PAT) has the potential to recover morphological and functional tissue properties with high spatial resolution. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical conclusion carried from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach to PAT image synthesis involves subdividing the challenge into probabilistic generation of realistic tissue morphology and pixel-wise assignment of corresponding optical and acoustic properties. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach to PAT image synthesis involves subdividing the challenge into probabilistic generation of realistic tissue morphology and pixel-wise assignment of corresponding optical and acoustic properties. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The methodological contribution is restated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) trained on semantically annotated medical imaging data achieves the generation of realistic tissue morphology. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) trained on semantically annotated medical imaging data achieves the generation of realistic tissue morphology. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result regarding GANs generating realistic morphology is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach yields more realistic synthetic images than the traditional model-based approach according to a validation study on a downstream task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach yields more realistic synthetic images than the traditional model-based approach according to a validation study on a downstream task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The validation result is restated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach combines measurements from event and frame-based cameras for 6 degree-of-freedom object motion tracking. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach combines measurements from event and frame-based cameras for 6 degree-of-freedom object motion tracking. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is carried into the publication verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The tracking is formulated using a probabilistic generative model of the event measurement process. (certainty=high, type=method)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tracking is formulated using a probabilistic generative model of the event measurement process. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The substance of the claim is unchanged; only the label/type shifted from 'method' to 'result' in the publication, but the content remains equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The object trajectory is refined in slower rate image frames through direct image alignment. (certainty=high, type=method)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The object trajectory is refined in slower rate image frames through direct image alignment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The procedural claim is preserved in content; the publication frames it as a reported result rather than merely a method.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy of the proposed approach is evaluated in several object tracking scenarios with synthetic data and real data. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy of the proposed approach is evaluated in several object tracking scenarios with synthetic data and real data. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Evaluation and its scope are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ten deep learning models of regression were developed for precise and robust prediction of future stock prices. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ten deep learning models of regression were developed for precise and robust prediction of future stock prices. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Q0 is verbatim the same contribution as P0 in wording and scope, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models were trained using granular stock price data collected at 5-minute intervals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models were trained using granular stock price data collected at 5-minute intervals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q1 repeats P1 exactly about training data granularity, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the models was analyzed based on accuracy in forecasting and speed of execution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the models was analyzed based on accuracy in forecasting and speed of execution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q2 restates P2 directly, so the analysis claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The research challenges the classical efficient market hypothesis by demonstrating the potential for accurate stock price predictions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include or restate the claim about challenging the efficient market hypothesis, so this conclusion was removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Collaborative board games present a different challenge to artificial intelligence compared to competitive board games. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Collaborative board games present unique challenges to artificial intelligence by requiring a balance between short-term risk mitigation and long-term winning strategies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Q0 restates P0 but adds specificity about the tension between short-term risk mitigation and long-term strategy, so the claim is clarified rather than changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The rolling horizon evolutionary algorithm designed for the game Pandemic incorporates specially designed forward models and macro-action representations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The rolling horizon evolutionary algorithm designed for the game Pandemic incorporates specially designed forward models and macroaction representations for decision making. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Q1 conveys the same contribution as P1 with only a minor wording addition (for decision making), so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An evolutionary approach via short-horizon rollouts can better account for future dangers in the game Pandemic compared to a baseline hierarchical policy agent. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evolutionary approach via short-horizon rollouts can better account for future dangers introduced by the board in collaborative board games. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q3 preserves the core finding about short-horizon rollouts handling future dangers but generalizes it from Pandemic to collaborative board games, broadening the scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results highlight the challenges that collaborative board games pose to artificial intelligence, particularly in managing multi-player collaboration interactions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Collaborative board games pose specific challenges for artificial intelligence, particularly in managing multiplayer collaboration interactions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Q4 restates P3's conclusion about multiplayer collaboration challenges with essentially the same scope and confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Variants of the evolutionary algorithm that explore different game state evaluations and mutation rates outperform a baseline hierarchical policy agent. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q2 reports an additional result about algorithm variants outperforming a baseline that is not present in the preprint, so it is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multimodal Transport Systems (MMTS) are a mainstream transportation method that integrates various transportation processes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multimodal Transport Systems (MMTS) are a mainstream transportation means that facilitate an integrative transportation process. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Q0 mirrors P0's assertion about MMTS being mainstream and integrative, with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The volume of goods and distances covered in transportation have doubled in the last ten years, indicating a high demand for optimized transportation solutions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific quantitative claim about volumes/distance doubling does not appear in the publication, so this result was removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a need to identify industry needs and gaps in multimodal transportation research to provide context for future studies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The survey identifies industry needs and research gaps in multimodal transportation research. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Q2 keeps the same identification of needs and gaps but reframes it as a survey contribution with reduced certainty, so the claim is clarified and confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The gap between theoretical aspects of multimodal transport and real-world applications needs to be addressed to optimize the global multimodal transportation system. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The gap between theory and real-world applications in multimodal transportation needs to be addressed to optimize the global system. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: Q3 matches P3's limitation but lowers the stated certainty, so the content is unchanged while confidence is tempered.

alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a high demand for optimized transportation that is fast, low-cost, resource-saving, and safe with low or zero emissions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q1 introduces an explicit claim about the types of demand for optimized transportation not present in the preprint, so it is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed ANN-SBO outperforms all three benchmark hybrids (i.e., ANN-HGSO, ANN-SFO, and ANN-VSA) in analyzing the UCS behavior of concrete. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ANN-SBO outperforms all three benchmark hybrids (i.e., ANN-HGSO, ANN-SFO, and ANN-VSA) in predicting uniaxial compressive strength. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q1 directly matches P0's comparative performance claim against the three benchmarks with equivalent wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The correlation indices and mean absolute percentage errors indicate that the ANN-SBO provides the best prediction performance among the tested models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ANN-SBO achieved the best prediction performance with correlation indices and mean absolute percentage errors that are superior to the benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q2 restates P1's performance metrics claim (correlation indices and MAPE) showing ANN-SBO's superiority, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ANN-VSA also achieved reliable results in predicting the UCS of concrete. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ANN-VSA also achieved reliable results in predicting the UCS of concrete. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q3 repeats P2's statement about ANN-VSA achieving reliable results, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ANN-SBO can be used by engineers as an efficient non-destructive method for predicting the UCS of concrete. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ANN-SBO can be used by engineers as an efficient non-destructive method for predicting the UCS of concrete. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Q4 is a direct restatement of P3 about practical use by engineers, so it is unchanged.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed ANN-SBO can excellently analyze the UCS behavior of concrete. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q0 is a general positive restatement of results present elsewhere in the paper but was not separately asserted in the preprint, so it appears as an added summary claim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach for learning on heterogeneous graphs does not rely on meta-paths. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach for learning on heterogeneous graphs does not rely on meta-paths. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Q0 is verbatim P0, so the contribution claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The decomposition of a heterogeneous graph into different homogeneous relation-type graphs preserves the heterogeneity of edges and retains their edge directions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The representations created by the proposed method preserve the heterogeneity of edges and retain their edge directions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q2 preserves P1's core claim about heterogeneity and edge directions but frames it in terms of the resulting representations, making the description slightly clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model outperforms other state-of-the-art baselines in the vertex classification task on three commonly studied heterogeneous graph datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that the proposed model outperforms other state-of-the-art baselines in the vertex classification task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q3 matches P2's experimental performance claim on vertex classification, with equivalent intent and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Attention mechanisms are used to distinguish the importance of the relation-type based neighbors and the relation-types themselves. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim explicitly restates the use of attention to distinguish neighbor and relation-type importance, so this conclusion appears omitted in the final paper.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method decomposes a heterogeneous graph into different homogeneous relation-type graphs to create higher-order relation-type representations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Q1 emphasizes the creation of higher-order relation-type representations from decomposition, an emphasis not explicitly stated in the preprint as a separate claim, so it is treated as an added publication-level contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed image translation method effectively addresses the issue of seasonal variation in remote sensing change detection. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed image translation method effectively addresses the issue of seasonal variation in remote sensing change detection. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the exact same contribution claim with no substantive change in wording or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A style-based recalibration module is introduced to effectively capture seasonal features. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A style-based recalibration module is introduced to effectively capture seasonal features. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this module contribution verbatim, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new style discriminator improves translation performance by producing decisions for fake or real samples and returning a style vector based on channel-wise correlations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new style discriminator improves translation performance by producing decisions for fake or real samples and returning a style vector based on channel-wise correlations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same description of the style discriminator and its role, so no change is detected.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed method consistently improves season-varying image change detection performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed method consistently improves season-varying image change detection performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental result claim is carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The existing multi-modal FAS datasets do not adequately address cross-domain problems under deployment scenarios. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of face anti-spoofing significantly degrades when images are low-resolution, blurry, and from different domains. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We constructed a cross-domain multi-modal FAS dataset under surveillance scenarios called GREAT-FASD-S. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GREAT-FASD-S dataset addresses the cross-domain problems in face anti-spoofing under surveillance scenarios. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We proposed an Attention based Face Anti-spoofing network with Feature Augment (AFA) to address FAS for low-quality face images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Attention based Face Anti-spoofing network with Feature Augment (AFA) can achieve state-of-the-art performance on the CASIA-SURF dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes experimental success (SOTA on CASIA-SURF) rather than only introducing the model; it therefore clarifies and narrows the original contribution toward a dataset-specific result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model achieves state-of-the-art performance on both the CASIA-SURF dataset and the GREAT-FASD-S dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: CrowdTeacher outperforms baseline methods that model individual annotations and combine them, as well as methods that learn a classifier while inferring truth labels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This preprint claim is merged with the previous comparison in the publication; the content is preserved but combined into a single, clarified sentence.

alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the robustness claim but adds a specific mechanism (prevents overfitting on easy features) and focuses on medical images, narrowing the original broader dataset claim while clarifying effects.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Networks trained with spectral decoupling achieve up to 9.5 percent point higher performance on external datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Networks trained with spectral decoupling achieve up to 9.5 percent point higher performance on external datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Spectral decoupling can be used to complement or replace computationally expensive explicit bias mitigation methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.rationale: The publication omits the broad prescriptive recommendation to use spectral decoupling for any clinical neural network, so this specific recommendation is removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A method for constructing word embeddings for endangered languages using existing word embeddings of resource-rich languages and translation dictionaries has been developed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A method for constructing word embeddings for endangered languages using existing word embeddings of resource-rich languages and translation dictionaries has been developed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The word embeddings for endangered languages are well-aligned with resource-rich languages and suitable for training task-specific models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The word embeddings for endangered languages are well-aligned with resource-rich languages. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sentiment analysis model built using cross-lingual word embeddings achieved high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All cross-lingual word embeddings and the sentiment analysis model have been released openly via an easy-to-use Python library. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed machine-learning framework can accurately predict the synchronization transition in dynamical systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning-based framework can predict the synchronization transition in coupled oscillators using a model-free and fully data-driven approach. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning scheme can predict the precise locations of transition points associated with forward and backward transition paths in systems exhibiting explosive transitions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning scheme can predict the locations of transition points and the hysteresis in systems exhibiting explosive (first-order) transitions. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prediction framework is model free and fully data driven, addressing the challenge of unknown system equations in real settings. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning-based framework can predict the synchronization transition in coupled oscillators using a model-free and fully data-driven approach. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed system demonstrates robust, high-accuracy (95-99%) and low-latency (50-120 msec) control of individual finger movements. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A nonlinear structure from motion (SFM) model is considered to obtain lateral and longitudinal distances in the context of moving cameras and objects. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A nonlinear structure from motion (SFM) model alongside a state-dependent Riccati equation (SDRE) filter is proposed to obtain lateral and longitudinal distances. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed switching method enhances the robust performance of the SDRE filter by utilizing switching estimation error covariance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: C-HMCNN(h) is a novel approach for solving hierarchical multi-label classification problems that improves performance by exploiting hierarchy information. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The logic used to express hierarchical multi-label classification constraints has been extended to specify more complex relations among classes. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CCN(h) extends C-HMCNN(h) and is capable of satisfying and exploiting constraints to further improve performance. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CCN(h) extends C-HMCNN(h) and is capable of satisfying and exploiting constraints to further improve performance. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Training data available only for discrete values of a continuous parameter can lead to performance-limiting issues and biased estimates. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method could successfully identify at least one fix commit for up to 84.03% of the vulnerabilities. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model effectively predicts health status based on a structure-aware representation learned from a local-global behavior graph. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed predictor structure based on the Kazantzis-Kravaris/Luenberger (KKL) observer fits well into the general framework for output prediction.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed solution for the predictor relies solely on a small set of trajectories measured from the system.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We present a new dataset termed F-SIOL-310 which is specifically captured for testing few-shot incremental object learning capability for robotic vision.

(certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a specific assumption (epigraph of the non-smooth term is polyhedral), which narrows the claim's applicability but does not change its stated result or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm has the same order of computation complexity as existing gradient tracking based methods but lower order of communication complexity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The presented result is the first stochastic algorithm with the $\text{O}(1/\text{ε})$ communication complexity for non-convex and non-smooth problems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
